name: Selective Autophagy and Organelle Quality Control Coverage Assessment for June 12, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Backfill assessment of the published June 2 selective-autophagy baseline and the June 5 no-op surveillance session; no new external source search was run for this assessment.

summary: Coverage is adequate for the current low-confidence human_functional_benefit public claim because the local map contains repeated urolithin A human intervention signals for muscle endurance or strength, human mitochondrial and immune-aging biomarker support, animal mitophagy and muscle-function proof-of-concept, USP30 mouse mitophagy tractability, and a 2026 MF-094 mutant-mtDNA boundary. Coverage is not strong because the human stage is short-duration, sponsor-heavy, and dominated by one supplement program; endpoint results are mixed; causal attribution to mitophagy or broader selective-organelle quality control is unresolved; USP30 inhibition remains preclinical or in vitro; safety and durability are not deeply mapped; active urolithin A registries remain no-results watch items; and recent-review or formal seminal-anchor triangulation has not been recorded. The June 5 surveillance pass found no material post-publication delta, so ordinary surveillance can continue.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes the main urolithin A human intervention anchors for the current public stage: first-in-human safety and mitochondrial signatures, ENERGIZE older-adult muscle-endurance and biomarker movement, ATLAS middle-aged muscle-strength and biomarker movement, and MitoImmune immune-cell composition, metabolism, and ex vivo function. The source-fidelity and interpretation reviews preserve the short-duration, mixed-endpoint, and commercial-program boundaries.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Adequacy is limited to the current narrow urolithin A functional-stage read; the map does not establish durable mobility, frailty, disease, infection, hospitalization, mortality, or broad selective-autophagy benefit.

evidence_categories.rationale: The assessed artifacts do not systematically map observational cohorts, natural experiments, dietary urolithin exposure, microbiome-conversion phenotypes, tissue mitophagy marker trajectories, or aging outcomes.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This is not blocking for the current intervention baseline, but future review could decide whether observational urolithin-production or organelle-quality-control evidence adds useful human relevance or attribution limits.

evidence_categories.rationale: The Ryu urolithin A source anchors preclinical mitophagy induction, C. elegans lifespan extension, and rodent muscle-function improvement. This is enough to support biological plausibility for the current public read while keeping mammalian lifespan and broad healthspan claims out of scope.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Animal coverage is not a substitute for replicated older-adult functional benefit, and the lifespan signal is non-mammalian.

evidence_categories.rationale: Mechanistic coverage spans urolithin A mitophagy and mitochondrial-health signatures, human skeletal-muscle and systemic biomarker movement, immune-cell metabolism and ex vivo function, USP30/ST-539 reporter-mouse mitophagy activation, and MF-094-induced mitochondrial ubiquitination and mitolysosome formation in cybrids.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Mitophagy-linked biomarkers do not prove that selective autophagy causally drove the human functional signals, and the track has little mature evidence outside mitophagy.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public outlook and reviews explicitly preserve the main limiting results: ENERGIZE did not significantly improve 6-minute walk distance or maximal ATP production, ATLAS did not significantly improve the primary peak-power endpoint, MitoImmune is biomarker and ex vivo function weighted rather than clinical-outcome evidence, and MF-094 induced mitophagy markers without significantly reducing mutant mtDNA load.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage does not include a broad null-results registry map as public records; the June 5 surveillance log instead records no-results or unchanged registry status for watch items.

evidence_categories.rationale: The local map represents short-term urolithin A safety in human trials and preserves commercial-conflict and endpoint caveats, but it does not deeply map chronic durability, frailty-specific safety, kidney or supplement-safety signals, long-term immune effects, target-selectivity risk, or chronic USP30 inhibition safety.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Do not strengthen confidence or LEV relevance without longer-duration functional, safety, and durability data tied to target engagement.

evidence_categories.rationale: The June 5 surveillance session checked baseline urolithin A registries and broader urolithin A, Mitopure, USP30, ST-539, MF-094, mitophagy, and mitochondrial-quality-control registry terms. Known urolithin A records remained completed or active without posted results, and NCT06274749 surfaced as recruiting with no results.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue to watch NCT06274749, NCT06556706, NCT07231783, NCT07060898, and related urolithin A registries, but current no-results registry context does not require coverage repair before surveillance resumes.

evidence_categories.rationale: The backfill reviewed local bootstrap and surveillance artifacts. The June 5 pass found a July 2025 human urolithin A systematic-review preprint and several review-level mitophagy or organelle-quality-control records as context, but no formal review or meta-analysis triangulation has been reconciled against the seven-source baseline.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A future source-completeness pass should compare recent urolithin A, mitophagy, USP30, sarcopenia, immune aging, and selective-autophagy reviews against the current source map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes plausible anchor records for urolithin A animal mitophagy, first-in-human safety and mitochondrial biomarkers, ENERGIZE, ATLAS, MitoImmune, ST-539 USP30 mouse mitophagy, and MF-094 USP30 cybrid limitations. It is not strong because no formal must-include, review-cited, or citation-network audit is recorded.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Formal anchor auditing could move this category from adequate to strong.

evidence_categories.rationale: The outlook, bootstrap notes, and interpretation review explicitly bound this track against mitophagy-enhancers, mitochondrial dysfunction, mitochondrial biogenesis, autophagy induction, nutrient sensing, microbiome-metabolite restoration, immune aging, and broader supplement or exercise-performance claims. USP30 is kept as mechanistic and boundary evidence rather than as support for the human stage.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Boundary maintenance remains important because future urolithin A, USP30, exercise, immune, microbiome, or mitochondrial-metabolism records may belong partly or primarily to neighboring tracks.

search_log_summary: This backfill reviewed local project artifacts: the published Selective Autophagy and Organelle Quality Control outlook, June 2 bootstrap session, published candidate bundle, source-fidelity and interpretation reviews, publication event, and June 5 no-op surveillance session with its PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, bioRxiv/medRxiv, correction/retraction, and web-search log. No new PubMed, registry, preprint, citation-network, or web source search was run for this assessment.
source_selection_notes: PMID 32694802, PMID 35050355, PMID 35584623, and PMID 41174221 are the human urolithin A anchors for safety, mitochondrial biomarkers, muscle endurance or strength, and immune-aging biomarker or ex vivo function context.
PMID 27400265 anchors preclinical urolithin A mitophagy, worm lifespan, and rodent muscle-function support.
PMID 33890401 and PMID 41587019 anchor USP30 targeted-mitophagy tractability and the mutant-mtDNA translation boundary.

The June 5 surveillance pass found no material post-publication source, no correction or retraction for the seven supporting PMIDs, no USP30 clinical result, and no posted urolithin A registry result.
NCT06274749, NCT06556706, NCT07231783, and NCT07060898 remain watch items rather than covered public sources because they had no posted results in the assessed surveillance log.

known_gaps.description: The public human_functional_benefit stage is driven almost entirely by short-duration urolithin A trials with mixed endpoint results and visible commercial-program concentration.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep confidence low and require independent, longer-duration, prespecified functional or clinical endpoint replication before strengthening the public stage.

known_gaps.description: Human mitochondrial, immune, and muscle-function signals do not prove that mitophagy or selective-organelle quality control causally drove the observed functional outcomes.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require target-engagement evidence that tracks with durable functional or disease-relevant benefit before making stronger selective-autophagy claims.

known_gaps.description: The current source map lacks long-lasting mobility, frailty, infection, vaccine-response, morbidity, hospitalization, mortality, and chronic safety or durability outcomes.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Do not assign a LEV-relevant timing claim or strengthen confidence until longer-term safety and clinically meaningful outcomes are available.

known_gaps.description: USP30 inhibition and other non-urolithin selective-autophagy or organelle-quality-control strategies remain preclinical, in vitro, or review-level for aging-relevant use.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep USP30 as mechanistic and limiting evidence until human target-engagement, safety, and functional data appear.

known_gaps.description: Urolithin A registry watch items such as NCT06274749, NCT06556706, NCT07231783, and NCT07060898 remain no-results or unchanged in the assessed surveillance log.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use ordinary surveillance to recheck these registries and linked publications for posted results or status changes.

known_gaps.description: Recent reviews, meta-analyses, and preprint systematic-review context around urolithin A, mitophagy, USP30, and selective autophagy have not been formally reconciled against the seven-source baseline.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use a future coverage-repair pass or surveillance review to compare recent reviews with the current source list and document missing decision-relevant records.

known_gaps.description: The same urolithin A and USP30 evidence touches mitophagy enhancers, microbiome-metabolite restoration, mitochondrial dysfunction, immune aging, supplement safety, nutrient sensing, and general exercise-performance contexts.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep future staged records explicit about whether they support selective autophagy, mitophagy, microbiome-derived metabolite replacement, mitochondrial metabolism, immune aging, or adjacent supplement claims.

next_coverage_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for Selective Autophagy and Organelle Quality Control. On the next pass, recheck urolithin A registries and publications, USP30 or other targeted-organelle-quality-control human translation, safety and durability signals, review-level context, and correction or retraction status; reserve coverage_repair for a future review-triangulation or formal seminal-anchor audit if no material field delta appears.
